reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Alex Pereira questionou os golpes recebidos contra Cyril Gane e criticou a atuação de Herb Dean. Já o árbitro respond
good: Hook creates immediate controversy—bold red text in Portuguese asks if the fight was 'stolen,' highly shareable premise
Creator's face fills frame immediately, personal reaction format drives connection
Shows actual fight footage and referee on screen, adds credibility to the narrative
Bilingual captions (Portuguese/English) broaden potential audience reach
bad: Only 11 saves despite 14.7k views—topic doesn't feel save-worthy; viewers aren't learning something actionable
190 shares is low for controversy content; suggests viewers aren't compelled to share the takeaway
Hook text entirely in Portuguese may confuse English-only viewers on initial scroll, losing momentum before the story lands
11.6s average watch time suggests drop-off early—pacing doesn't sustain curiosity from hook to resolution
reels.top_tip: English-language text in the hook is critical—translate 'Poatan teve a sua luta roubada?' to English or add English overlay so both audiences stay engaged; the controversy angle is strong but the narrative needs to resolve faster (get to the ref's response or the explanation by 5s to hold viewers through the decision moment)

reels.caption:
🇧🇷 Topuria realmente errou ou o Gaethje foi o responsável por criar todos os problemas dentro do octógono? 👀🔥
Nesse trec
good: Strong curiosity hook with debate framing ('Ego or Error?') in bold red/white text—creates immediate intrigue
Excellent pull-through metrics: 21.3% engagement rate and 9.3s average watch time indicate solid content retention
Bilingual captions (Portuguese + English) expand audience reach across Spanish-speaking MMA markets
Professional UFC branding and credible guest (Joanderson Brito) establish authority
bad: Weak shareability: only 185 shares and 3 saves despite 5.5K views—debate content should perform higher
Missing explicit call-to-action for comments; debate content lives in the comments but you're not prompting it
YouTube CTA at end drives traffic off-platform; Instagram favors watch-time-in-app
Mid-section underutilizes the debate angle—could use split-screen or graphics comparing the fighters directly
reels.top_tip: Add a direct comment prompt like 'Quem você acha?' (Who do you think?) overlaid during the debate discussion—your 21.3% engagement rate proves viewers want to discuss, but they need the prompt to convert into comments and shares

reels.caption:
🇺🇸 The odds point to a clear favorite. But in MMA, one punch can change everything.
Are you backing the champion or call
good: Strong hook with betting angle — 'WHO ARE YOU BETTING ON?' creates immediate prediction prompt that people want to debate and share
Excellent share rate (9.5%) — prediction/opinion format is inherently shareable across friend groups
Professional card design — fighter records, rankings, and matchup clarity are easy to parse
Bilingual captions (EN/PT) — expands reach to both English and Portuguese-speaking MMA audiences
High engagement rate (32.7%) — CTA strategy is working; viewers responding to prediction prompts
bad: Extremely low save rate (0.07%) — content isn't repeatable/reference-worthy; viewers consume and discard
No fight action visible — entire video is predictions and talking heads with zero actual footage, leaving engagement on the table
No retention loop — content ends after asking opinion; nothing encourages viewers to return or watch series
reels.top_tip: Add 2–3 seconds of actual fight highlight footage mid-video to break up the talking-head format and increase watch time. Then use a text overlay after the CTA ('See full breakdown in comments') to make the content feel like part of a series, not a one-off prediction—this drives saves and repeat views.

reels.caption: 🇺🇸 Ngannou made more in one boxing fight than his UFC career. McGregor lost to Floyd and still made around $100 million.
good: Excellent average watch time (29.1s) shows content holds attention throughout—narrative arc about fighter earnings is compelling
Strong hook contrast: bold red 'PAYS BETTER' text on dark background stops scroll immediately
Bilingual captions reach dual audience (English + Portuguese) with clear messaging about UFC vs boxing earnings
High engagement rate (24.3%) indicates audience finds topic discussion-worthy and wants to interact
bad: Low share-to-view ratio (15 shares from 1,269 views) despite good retention—content isn't compelling enough for peer sharing
Minimal saves (2) suggests viewers don't see actionable/valuable takeaway worth bookmarking
No visible call-to-action (tag someone, ask question, follow for more) to convert viewers into sharers
First frame lacks audio/motion cues visible—text-only hook misses opportunity for sound design or quick action clip to amplify scroll-stop
reels.top_tip: Add explicit social CTA in final frame: 'Tag a boxer who should know this' or 'Which fighter made better choices?' to convert 24% engagement into shares—you have the hook and retention, just need the push to make it viral

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Mais um card com o Brasil representado no UFC. Alguns nomes buscam subir no ranking, outros tentam manter o momento.
good: Strong hook with fighter names in bold red text and immediate heavy bag action—highly visible and scroll-stopping
Solid 44.5% engagement rate and 5.8s average watch time indicate the hook effectively captures attention
Multiple Brazilian fighters showcased (Andre Lima, Bia Mesquita, Vinicius Oliveira) provides variety and appeals to niche audience
Clear call-to-action in caption ('Quem você vê saindo com a vitória?') encourages prediction comments
bad: Extremely low saves (2) and shares (33/993 reach = 3.3%) despite good viewership—content lacks save/share incentive
Mid-section shifts to training drills and interview footage instead of fight action, breaking momentum built by hook
Long bilingual caption dilutes primary message and may reduce CTA clarity for primary audience
No narrative payoff or prediction/controversy angle—viewers watch but have no reason to reshare
reels.top_tip: Replace mid-video training segments with fight footage or add a bold prediction/hot take overlay (e.g., 'Who gets upset?' or a fighter ranking statement) to drive shares and saves—you've nailed the hook, now give people a reason to reshare

reels.caption:
🇺🇸 Conor McGregor and Max Holloway are already fighting long before July.
The punches haven't started yet.The mind games
good: Hook uses confrontational Portuguese quotes that create immediate intrigue and stop scrolls
Bilingual text (Portuguese/English) expands reach across multiple language audiences in MMA
High engagement rate (73.1%) and share count (23) indicate memorable, quotable moments viewers want to distribute
Clear narrative progression from trash talk through weight class reality builds tension effectively
bad: Low reach (323) despite strong engagement — algorithm not distributing widely in initial pool despite good performance
Minimal saves (2) shows content entertains but doesn't feel bookmark-worthy or actionable
No visible call-to-action — missing direction for engagement (comment prediction, follow, etc.)
Audio cues not evident in frames — lacks dramatic fight sounds, music, or announcer voice for sensory amplification
reels.top_tip: Add explicit CTA text overlay (e.g., 'Who wins? Comment 🔥') to increase algorithmic signals, convert passive viewing to interaction, and boost reach on initial distribution

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Cutelaba aposta na pressão e no perigo dos primeiros minutos. Stirling chega invicto, confiando na técnica e na movim
good: Exceptional engagement rate (67.3%) proves narrative structure resonates—viewers stay for full arc
Hook delivers intense personal moment + clear MMA prediction text overlay, avoiding scroll ambiguity
Strong watch-through time (7.9s) indicates pacing and story progression hold attention effectively
Bilingual captions (Portuguese + English) expand addressable audience for broader reach
Clear three-act structure: matchup preview → character/respect moment → fight action → prediction
bad: Only 1 save from 739 views—viewers consume but don't perceive the content as reference-worthy or save-worthy
Low share rate (~3.2%) for prediction-focused sports content; fight fans should share bold predictions more readily
Prediction payoff ('Cutelaba by early stoppage') comes at the end; moving it forward would increase viral coefficient
Multiple overlapping text layers in hook may feel cluttered on small mobile screens, delaying message clarity
reels.top_tip: Front-load the prediction to the hook ('Cutelaba by stoppage') instead of revealing it at the end—MMA audiences share bold predictions, not emotional moments. Use the respect/character moment as supporting evidence in the middle, not the opening hook.

reels.caption: 🇺🇸 From kickboxing to the UFC, Poatan has built one of the most terrifying knockout resumes in combat sports. These are
good: Strong hook: 'TOP 5 KNOCKOUTS' with fighter action visible immediately creates instant stop potential
Progressive ranked reveal strategy maintains viewer momentum and compels watching to completion
Bilingual caption (English/Portuguese) expands reach to larger audience segments
Excellent retention metrics: 20% engagement rate and 4.9s average watch time indicate strong content resonance
bad: Critical viral bottleneck: only 1 share and 2 saves from 1152 views shows content isn't being passed along despite good initial engagement
Video ends with #1 knockout unrevealed—creates frustration instead of satisfaction, actively discouraging shares/saves
Hook text size adequate but could be 15-20% larger for more aggressive scroll-stop impact
Missing aggressive CTA for sharing (e.g., 'Tag your fight crew' or 'Share if [fighter] is your GOAT') limits pass-along driver
reels.top_tip: Reveal the #1 knockout as a dramatic climax with powerful visual impact—the cliffhanger currently frustrates rather than compels sharing. Ending with an impressive KO moment + direct share CTA would convert high engagement into viral amplification.

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Um traz poder explosivo. O outro traz experiência e uma vitória no confronto direto. Uma disputa de cinturão pode est
good: Strong visual hook: fighter faces fill frame immediately with bold contrast text overlay
Action-focused framing with clear octagon setting maintains production quality throughout
Bilingual captions (Portuguese + English) expand reach across audiences
bad: Zero shares despite 658 views indicates preview lacks shareability — no clear prediction or debate angle to drive discussion
5.8s average watch suggests significant drop-off before the narrative payoff or resolution
Minimal saves (1) signals preview doesn't compel viewers to bookmark for fight day
Text sequence ('could decide' → 'dangerous' → 'beaten him once') lacks a clear call-to-action or finishing hook
reels.top_tip: End with explicit narrative payoff: add a clear prediction or stat ('Kape's explosive power vs. Horiguchi's 2-0 head-to-head') + direct CTA ('Who takes it?') to drive shares and extend watch time past 5.8s
